steps.title: Determine the slope
steps.content: The line increases from lower-left to upper-right, indicating a positive slope. From $x = 0$ to $x = 10$, the line rises from about $y = 3.4$ to $y = 13.4$, giving a slope of $$\frac{10}{10} = 1$$ This eliminates C and D, which have slope $−1$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $y \approx 3.4$. Since the $y$-intercept is positive, choice B (with intercept $−3.4$) is eliminated. With slope 1 and $y$-intercept 3.4, the equation is $$y = x + 3.4$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
